rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: ChromeLoader NW.js Runtime App Installation Paths

rules.rule_text:
title: ChromeLoader NW.js Runtime App Installation Paths
id: be3e4b3e-4076-4ae3-acb9-3713c651158b
status: experimental
description: "Red Canary has had success detecting ChromeLoader by looking for instances\
  \ of NW.js \napplications that are unsigned and running from %AppData/Roaming%.\
  \ This behavior is \nnot unique to ChromeLoader, and some tuning may be required\
  \ to avoid detecting \nlegitimate applications. A partial list of common ChromeLoader\
  \ NW.js runtime app \ninstallation paths is included below to help distinguish the\
  \ signal from the noise. \nPart of the RedCanary 2024 Threat Detection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/threats/chromeloader/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.persistence
- attack.t1176
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection1:
    Image|re: ^.*:\\users\\.*\\appdata\\roaming\\.*$
  selection2:
    Image|endswith:
    - \bloom\bloom.exe
    - \cash/\cash.exe
    - \diet\diet.exe
    - \editor\editor.exe
    - \healthy\healthy.exe
    - \leading\leading.exe
    - \movies\movies.exe
    - \music\music.exe
    - \prime\prime.exe
    - \player\player.exe
    - \tone\tone.exe
    - \videos\videos.exe
    - \copy\nw.exe
    - \coreelevate\nw.exe
    - \debuggerstepperboundaryattribute\nw.exe
  condition: all of selection*
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
